name: Southern Yukaghir

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Southern Yukaghir (sout2750).

notes: Skeleton. Southern (Kolyma) Yukaghir is a Yukaghir language of northeastern Siberia written in a Cyrillic alphabet extended for its palatal and back vowels. The map covers the base Cyrillic letters plus Ӈ (velar nasal), Ө (front rounded vowel) and the palatal digraphs нь (ɲ) and дь (dʲ); в is rendered as the approximant w. Yukaghir has phonemic vowel length and vowel harmony that the orthography does not consistently mark, and the reference gold is a narrow transcription, so length, harmony and finer allophony are left unencoded and a substantial PER floor is expected.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Southern Yukaghir (sout2750)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
